description: Responsive layout audit and fixes. Fix overflow issues, ensure proper image sizing, add min-w-0 to flex containers, and verify layouts at key breakpoints (375, 390, 414, 768, 820, 1024, 1280). Fix horizontal scroll sections causing page-level overflow. Replace any fixed px widths with relative/responsive values.
steps: Fix components/product-image.tsx - currently uses raw tag with width=640 height=640 attributes. Since this renders product images at various container sizes, ensure it has proper intrinsic sizing. The parent containers set the height, so the img fills correctly. Add aspect-ratio to the img for CLS prevention. Consider converting to next/image with fill prop for proper responsive sizing, OR keep as but ensure the parent has explicit dimensions.

In components/home/hero-section.tsx - the desktop hero uses motion.img (raw img tag) for heroImage. Replace with next/image using fill prop and sizes prop for proper responsive behavior, or at minimum add explicit width/height/aspect-ratio. The mobile hero also uses motion.img - same fix.

In components/product-grid.tsx - the category chips and filter buttons use overflow-x-auto which is correct. Ensure the parent containers have overflow-hidden or overflow-x-clip on the page level to prevent horizontal page scroll. The .scroll-x and .wheel-scroll classes in globals.css use overscroll-behavior-x: contain which is good.
Audit app/page.tsx - the desktop grid uses lg:grid-cols-[1fr_380px]. The 380px is fine as it's within a max-w-7xl container. The mobile section uses grid-cols-2 gap-2.5 which is responsive-safe.

In components/home/animated-product-section.tsx - wheel-scroll-item has fixed widths w-[155px] sm:w-[170px] md:w-[200px]. These are fine since they are inside a scroll container. But ensure the scroll container doesn't cause page overflow by adding overflow-hidden on a parent or ensuring the section has overflow-hidden.
Check the html element overflow-x: clip in globals.css (already set). Verify body also has overflow-x: clip (already set). This prevents any child from causing horizontal page scroll.

Add min-w-0 to flex containers in components/cart/cart-page-client.tsx where text might overflow (the item name truncation). The flex-1 min-w-0 pattern is already used in most places - verify it's consistent.

In components/header.tsx - verify desktop nav links and right actions don't overflow on 768px tablet width. The hidden lg:flex on nav means it only shows at 1024px+. At 768px only the mobile header shows. Good.
Ensure components/home/promo-banners.tsx banner cards don't overflow on 375px - the flex gap-3 layout with flex-1 and w-24 shrink-0 is safe.
acceptance_criteria: No horizontal page-level scroll at any of the target widths (375, 390, 414, 768, 820, 1024, 1280)
All images have explicit dimensions or aspect-ratio to prevent layout shift
Flex containers with text content use min-w-0 to prevent overflow
No TypeScript or lint errors introduced
verification: Run npx next lint to confirm no lint errors
Run npx tsc --noEmit to confirm no type errors
